observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and other relevant ledger cards to record the details of the PPEs found in the inventory control account in the general ledger; and submit Subsidiary Ledgers in support of the amount in the General Ledger;
Direct the GSO to ensure that the properties and equipment delivered are immediately recorded in the respective control property cards based on the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) and other supporting documents; and
Implement the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the Accounts Payable outstanding for more than 2 years to Unappropriated Surplus (Government Equity), in which no actual claim, administrative and judicial has been filed, in consonance with the provisions of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to evaluate properly the validity of the cause of the contractor’s delay to determine whether time extensions granted falls within the critical of the PERT/CPM network (Item 11, Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184); and
Impose liquidated damages, if warranted, as a result of the delayed completion of the project in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and the contract agreement between the LGU Naawan and Jejor’s Construction Corporation before final payment is made in the project; and
Regularly monitor accomplishment of the contractor in consonance with the timeline of construction requiring the updated Statement of Work Accomplished to establish the correct status of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor periodically the liquation of the cash advances in accordance with the reglementary periods prescribed under COA Circulars to ensure timely accounting and recording thereof and to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
Compel all concerned employees to strictly comply with provisions of the Circulars pertaining to cash advances submitting immediately the necessary documents and records to fully account and liquidate the cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct Bids and Awards Committee to establish a legal document or contract defining the terms and conditions of the lease and the rights and obligations of the parties, such as the determination of measurement of rental revenue at fair value; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize accrual of rental income of government-owned property to properly realize the rights and obligations of the government and to prevent putting its interest into disadvantage. Subsequently, collection be made from these accruals.

We recommended that management instruct:

Require the Human Resource Management Office to keep and maintain an updated record of the earned leaves of all officials and employees of the LGU at year-end and furnish the Municipal Accountant a summary of earned leave credits with monetary amount as basis in recording the accrued benefits; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up as of the year-end adjusting entries of accrued liability the leave benefits payable of all officials and employees and include a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement the details of such amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management, the following measures in compliance to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 and Section 2 of PD 1445:

a.)	To direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of directly recording purchased construction materials to Construction-in-Progress accounts and see to it that they are properly recorded to their corresponding inventory account; 

b.)	To direct the designated Property Custodian to monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories, and maintain the monitoring forms. Issuances shall be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which will be summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to Municipal Mayor to enjoin the following department heads compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No.  2015-008:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should dig up their records of assets previously closed to RPI to establish the same.
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

After ensuring the reliability of the accumulated leave credit balances of employees as of December 31, 2019, require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to record the Accrued Terminal Leave Benefits due to the employees; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should take proper action on government properties that are for disposal. The General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require all the employees with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2017 to immediately submit liquidation reports by sending them demand letters. Otherwise, the Local Chief Executive will be advised to cause or order the withholding of the payment of any money due the employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the conduct of an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Road Networks account.

We also recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed and the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency.

Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant commented that she is still in process of retrieving the Registry of Public Infrastructure which contains the data on Local Road Networks that were previously transferred from the books. Once the registry is retrieved, it shall be reconciled with the actual inventory of local roads to ensure proper and accurate accounting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must ensure that programs/projects funded out of DA-BUB should be implemented so that the intended beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits of the projects, taking into consideration that these funds were received by the LGU as early as August 31, 2015. The concerned LGU Employees should facilitate implementation of the said projects to fully liquidate the fund transfers.

Management Comment
The management commented during the exit conference that the implementation of the remaining projects is already on going.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should coordinate with the donor-agency to secure the deed of donation or the bill of lading/airway bill/parcel notice and other related documents which contains the declared values of the donated PPEs as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We also recommended that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2014-002 on in-kind donations be strictly followed and the required reports be submitted to this office and the donated PPEs should be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency.

Management Comment
Management commented that upon coordination with the MDRRM Office with the World Food Program to secure the said documents, the former was informed that there were no such documents. As there are no documents that contain the declared values of the donated PPEs as basis for recording in the books of accounts, the Inventory Committee will make an appraisal report of the donated PPEs and the same will be used by the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording in the books of accounts. The guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2014-002 on in-kind donations will also be strictly followed.
